title: Bangkok 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Bangkok 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Bangkok, Thailand: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Grand Palace & Wat Phra Kaew and Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn). Prices from $2079 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Bangkok, Thailand — Tropical climate, hot year-round with highs 31-35°C; the hot season (March-May) is intense, the rainy season (June-October) brings frequent afternoon downpours, and November-February is the most pleasant period. November to February (cool, dry season) offers the most comfortable temperatures and humidity for exploring temples and street markets. This itinerary blends Grand Palace & Wat Phra Kaew, Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn), Chatuchak Weekend Market (browsing) with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Grand Palace & Wat Phra Kaew, Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn), Chatuchak Weekend Market (browsing)
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Bangkok
itinerary.description: Land at Suvarnabhumi Airport (BKK) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Grand Palace & Wat Phra Kaew — Former royal residence and Temple of the Emerald Buddha. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Bangkok: Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn) & Wat Pho (Reclining Buddha)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn) — Riverside temple with a distinctive porcelain-inlaid spire. In the afternoon, continue to Wat Pho (Reclining Buddha) — Temple housing a 46-meter gold reclining Buddha. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Bangkok: Chao Phraya river dinner cruise & Jim Thompson House
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Chao Phraya river dinner cruise — Evening cruise past illuminated riverside temples. In the afternoon, continue to Jim Thompson House — Traditional Thai house museum of the "Silk King". Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Ayutthaya ancient capital
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Ayutthaya ancient capital, a popular side trip from Bangkok. Return to Bangkok in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Ayutthaya ancient capital

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Bang Krachao "green lung" cycling island — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Bangkok: Damnoen Saduak floating market tour & Mahanakhon SkyWalk
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Damnoen Saduak floating market tour — Classic boat tour through a floating market. In the afternoon, continue to Mahanakhon SkyWalk — Glass-floor observation deck atop a skyscraper. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Damnoen Saduak floating market
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Damnoen Saduak floating market, a popular side trip from Bangkok. Return to Bangkok in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Damnoen Saduak floating market

itinerary.title: Bangkok: Muay Thai match at Rajadamnern Stadium & Ayutthaya day tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Muay Thai match at Rajadamnern Stadium — Live traditional Thai boxing matches. In the afternoon, continue to Ayutthaya day tour — Ancient ruined capital, UNESCO World Heritage Site. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Muay Thai match at Rajadamnern Stadium

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Talad Noi street art alleys — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Maeklong Railway Market
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Maeklong Railway Market, a popular side trip from Bangkok. Return to Bangkok in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Maeklong Railway Market

itinerary.title: Bangkok: Thai cooking class & Chatuchak Weekend Market (browsing)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Thai cooking class — Hands-on class covering classic Thai dishes. In the afternoon, continue to Chatuchak Weekend Market (browsing) — One of the world's largest weekend markets. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Bangkok: Khao San Road & Lumpini Park
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Khao San Road — Backpacker street famous for its nightlife and street food. In the afternoon, continue to Lumpini Park — Central park popular for jogging and monitor lizard sightings. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Wat Paknam Bhasicharoen glowing crystal pagoda — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Wat Paknam Bhasicharoen glowing crystal pagoda

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Chinatown (Yaowarat) or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to BKK for your departure flight.

travelTips: Use the BTS/MRT for most journeys — Bangkok traffic can turn a short taxi ride into an hour-long ordeal.
Insist on the meter in taxis, or use the Grab app to avoid price haggling.
Dress appropriately for the Grand Palace — rental sarongs are available on-site if needed but queues form.
Try street food from stalls with high turnover and visible cooking — it's some of the best food in the city and generally very safe.
Avoid single-use tuk-tuk "sightseeing" offers quoting suspiciously low prices — they usually detour to gem/tailor shops for commission.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Modest clothing with shoulders/knees covered for temple visits
Lightweight, breathable fabrics for intense heat and humidity
A reusable water bottle (tap water isn't potable)
A light rain jacket during the June-October wet season
thingsToDo: Muay Thai match at Rajadamnern Stadium
thingsToAvoid: The "Grand Palace is closed today" tuk-tuk scam — it's virtually always open; verify independently and ignore unsolicited claims.
Gem and tailor shop scams pitched by friendly strangers or tuk-tuk drivers offering suspiciously cheap tours.
Disrespecting Buddha imagery — never climb on statues or point your feet toward them for photos.
Riverside restaurants with aggressive street touts and no posted menu prices.
Drinking tap water or using it to brush teeth — stick to bottled or filtered water.
topRestaurants: Thipsamai Pad Thai — Thai Street Food ($)
Gaggan Anand — Progressive Indian Fine Dining ($$$$)
Or Tor Kor Market food stalls — Thai Street Food ($)
Sirocco Sky Bar restaurant — Mediterranean Rooftop Fine Dining ($$$$)
Raan Jay Fai-style street carts — Thai Street Food ($)
instagramSpots: Wat Arun at sunset from across the river
hiddenGems: Wat Paknam Bhasicharoen glowing crystal pagoda
Khlong Bang Luang artist canal community
luxuryExperiences: Fine dining at Gaggan Anand or Jay Fai
Private Chao Phraya river cruise on a heritage rice barge
Rooftop cocktails at Sirocco Sky Bar
Spa day at a 5-star riverside resort
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset dinner cruise on the Chao Phraya River
Private rooftop dinner with skyline views
Couples Thai massage and spa ritual
Evening tour of illuminated Wat Arun by boat
budgetExperiences: Street food crawl in Chinatown for a few dollars a dish
Free public ferry rides along the Chao Phraya
Lumpini Park for a free morning walk
localEtiquette: Never touch anyone's head or point your feet at people, monks, or Buddha images — both are considered highly disrespectful.
Dress modestly with shoulders and knees covered when visiting temples like the Grand Palace.
The wai (palms-together bow) is the traditional greeting; a slight bow back is polite when received.
Remove shoes before entering temples and many homes.
The monarchy is deeply revered — avoid any critical remarks about the royal family, which carries legal consequences.
localTransportGuide: The BTS Skytrain and MRT subway efficiently bypass Bangkok's notorious traffic for most tourist areas — a Rabbit Card makes fare payment seamless. Tuk-tuks and metered taxis (or Grab app) fill in the gaps, and the Chao Phraya Express boat is a scenic, practical way to reach riverside temples.
bestTimeToVisit: November to February (cool, dry season) offers the most comfortable temperatures and humidity for exploring temples and street markets.
weather: Tropical climate, hot year-round with highs 31-35°C; the hot season (March-May) is intense, the rainy season (June-October) brings frequent afternoon downpours, and November-February is the most pleasant period.
visaInformation: Most Western nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens can enter visa-free for tourism (typically up to 30-60 days depending on nationality and entry method). Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.
averageFlightTime: ~10.2h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Bangkok?
faqs.answer: Most Western nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens can enter visa-free for tourism (typically up to 30-60 days depending on nationality and entry method). Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Bangkok?
faqs.answer: November to February (cool, dry season) offers the most comfortable temperatures and humidity for exploring temples and street markets. Tropical climate, hot year-round with highs 31-35°C; the hot season (March-May) is intense, the rainy season (June-October) brings frequent afternoon downpours, and November-February is the most pleasant period.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Bangkok safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Bangkok is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Bangkok?
faqs.answer: The local currency is THB. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Anna Ibrahim
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Bangkok is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Ayutthaya day tour. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Daniel Fernandez
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Asiatique The Riverfront and Thai cooking class. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Mia Müller
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Wat Traimit exterior and Muay Thai match at Rajadamnern Stadium. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Ivanov
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Wat Traimit exterior alone was worth the trip, but combined with Jim Thompson House this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Costa
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Mahanakhon SkyWalk alone was worth the trip, but combined with Damnoen Saduak floating market tour this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: David Larsen
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Bangkok is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Chao Phraya river ferry ride. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Arjun van Dijk
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Booked this as a family of 4 trip and it was well paced. Lumpini Park in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Ava Smith
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
